steps.title: Identify the clause type
steps.content: The sentence is a declarative statement: "his voice acting work is why ______". The blank is a noun clause completing the predicate — it must use normal subject-verb order and end with a period.

steps.title: Apply word-order and punctuation rules for embedded clauses
steps.content:
Rule: a noun clause embedded in a declarative sentence uses statement word order (subject then verb) and the sentence ends with a period, not a question mark.
  • Choice A — "has he become such a celebrated talent?": inverted word order (auxiliary first) signals a question, but this is not a standalone question. Also ends with "?". ✗
  • Choice B — "has he become such a celebrated talent.": inverted word order is still wrong for an embedded clause, even with a period. ✗
  • Choice C — "he has become such a celebrated talent?": correct word order, but "?" is wrong for a declarative sentence. ✗
  • Choice D — "he has become such a celebrated talent.": statement word order (he + verb) and a period — both correct for a declarative noun clause. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
